w20: Shared-core compute instance featuring AMD EPYC Genoa architecture, 16 GB RAM, and 320 GB SSD for general-purpose application hosting.
c150: Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is a shared-core compute instance featuring an AMD EPYC Genoa processor, 16 GB of RAM, and 320 GB of local SSD storage for general use.
c200: The Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is a compute-optimized instance utilizing 8 shared vCPUs on AMD EPYC Genoa architecture, 16 GB of memory, and 320 GB of SSD storage, suitable for various general-purpose workloads.
w150: The Hetzner Cloud cpx42 is a compute-focused instance powered by an AMD EPYC Genoa x86_64 architecture. It provides 8 shared virtual CPUs operating at 2.0 GHz, supported by 16 GB of memory and 320 GB of local SSD storage. The CPU configuration includes 256 KB L1d, 8 MB L2, and 32 MB L3 cache, with support for AVX, AVX512F, AVX512_BF16, AES, and SHA-NI instruction sets. Benchmarks indicate strong single-core performance, placing it in the top 10 percent for stress-ng single-core tests and top 25 percent for Geekbench single-core scores. Memory bandwidth for large block read and write operations also ranks in the top 25 percent. This instance does not include a GPU. Given the balanced hardware profile and performance metrics, the cpx42 is suitable for general-purpose computing, web serving, database operations, and software compilation tasks where shared compute resources are sufficient for the required workload.
